items.title: Somalia: Somalia Says Turkish F-16 Airstrike Kills 35 Al-Shabab Fighters
items.description: [Shabelle] Mogadishu -- Somalia's military, backed by Turkish forces, carried out airstrikes using F-16 fighter jets on Tuesday targeting al-Shabab positions in the Godey area of the Lower Shabelle region, the Ministry of Defence said.
items.titleIt: Somalia: Somalia Says Turkish F-16 Airstrike Kills 35 Al-Shabab Fighters

items.title: EU imposes visa restrictions on Somalis after readmission row with Mogadishu
items.description: The European Union is imposing new restrictions on visas for Somali nationals after a disagreement with Mogadishu over the return of irregular migrants to their home country.
items.titleIt: EU imposes visa restrictions on Somalis after readmission row with Mogadishu

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Sudan, South Sudan and Somalia: Key Peace and Security Priorities for Africa in 2026
items.description: In this online event, Crisis Group experts discussed peace and security priorities for the African continent in 2026, zooming in on Somalia, Sudan and South Sudan ahead of the African Union's summit of heads of state.
items.titleIt: Sudan, South Sudan and Somalia: Key Peace and Security Priorities for Africa in 2026

items.title: Kenya: Cabinet Okays Kenya-U.S. Health Deal to Strengthen Disease Surveillance
items.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Kenya and the United States are set to deepen cooperation in public health after the Cabinet approved a new bilateral health framework.
items.titleIt: Kenya: Cabinet Okays Kenya-U.S. Health Deal to Strengthen Disease Surveillance

items.title: Italy-Ethiopia Agreement on Economic Reforms and Climate-Resilient Development
items.description: (ANSA) - NAIROBI, 30 GIU - The Ethiopian and Italian governments have signed a 70-million-euro financing package to support the macroeconomic reforms underway in Ethiopia and the country’s sustainable development agenda. The agreement was formally signed by Ethiopian Finance Minister Ahmed Shide and the Italian Ambassador to Ethiopia, Sem Fabrizi. The financing package, a testament to the strength and depth of the historic partnership between Ethiopia and Italy—founded on mutual trust and shared development priorities within the framework of the Mattei Plan for Africa— includes a €70 million c
items.titleIt: Italy-Ethiopia Agreement on Economic Reforms and Climate-Resilient Development

items.title: DRC opposition leader Sesanga banned from leaving the country
items.description: An opposition leader in the Democratic Republic of Congo said Monday he had been banned from leaving the country and stripped of his passport, condemning it as "intimidation" amid tensions with the government.
items.titleIt: DRC opposition leader Sesanga banned from leaving the country

items.title: Soccer: Nico Paz to stay at Como
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 26 - 21-year-old Argentina attacking midfielder Nico Paz is to stay on loan at Serie A club Como after they reached an agreement with his owner Real Madrid, the Lariani said Friday. The news, circulating from Spain, had been in the air, but an agreement between the Como club and Real Madrid was reportedly reached in the last few hours. The Spanish club will, however, maintain control over the player, with a buy-back clause of €80 million. The overall deal involves approximately €60 million for Como, which aims to make the buy-back clause valid only for the next summer transf
items.titleIt: Soccer: Nico Paz to stay at Como

items.title: As war keeps spreading, the African Union must find its teeth
items.description: With the continental body at its weakest point since its inception, this week’s summit in Ethiopia cannot be another talk shop. A united front is crucial to resolve intensifying conflicts tearing through Africa.
items.titleIt: As war keeps spreading, the African Union must find its teeth

items.title: Egypt: Egypt Sweat On Salah Fitness Ahead of Historic World Cup Clash With Australia
items.description: [CAF] Egypt are facing major injury concerns ahead of their Round of 32 clash with Australia at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with captain Mohamed Salah among three key players being monitored by the medical team.
items.titleIt: Egypt: Egypt Sweat On Salah Fitness Ahead of Historic World Cup Clash With Australia
